meaning: n.(根据前提得出的)推断,推理,结论
examples: Had she missed something important, or was this just a non sequitur?
The first one is a non-sequitur.
Let it stand as a picture, a visual non sequitur.
Nearly half of us were trying to figure out what this apparent non-sequitur had to do with our failing firm.
This is a non sequitur as if the wealthy paying higher rates are going to do something about the real problem.
